cases.passage: In a physics lab, students are tasked with determining the resultant displacement vector of a moving object. The object has been displaced 5 meters in the x-direction and 3 meters in the y-direction. Another force acting on the object results in a displacement of 2 meters in the x-direction and 1 meter in the y-direction. Determine the resultant displacement vector of the object. Assume the unit of displacement is in meters.

questions.q: What are the two displacement vectors in this scenario?
questions.explanation: The two displacement vectors are 5i + 3j and 2i + j.

questions.q: How do you find the resultant displacement vector?
opts: (A) By adding the corresponding components of the two vectors
(B) By multiplying the magnitudes of the two vectors
(C) By subtracting the corresponding components of the two vectors
(D) By dividing the magnitudes of the two vectors
questions.explanation: The resultant displacement vector is found by adding the corresponding components of the two vectors.

questions.q: What is the resultant displacement vector of the object?
What is the magnitude of the resultant displacement vector?
questions.explanation: The magnitude of the resultant displacement vector is √(7^2 + 4^2)

questions.q: What is the direction of the resultant displacement vector?
questions.explanation: The direction of the resultant displacement vector is Tan^-1(4/7)

cases.passage: A force of 10 N acts on an object in the x-direction. Another force of 20 N acts on the same object in the y-direction. Determine the resultant force vector acting on the object.

questions.q: What are the two force vectors in this scenario?
questions.explanation: The two force vectors are 10i + 20j and 10i - 20j.

questions.q: How do you find the resultant force vector?
opts: (A) By adding the corresponding components of the two vectors
(B) By multiplying the magnitudes of the two vectors
(C) By subtracting the corresponding components of the two vectors
(D) By dividing the magnitudes of the two vectors
questions.explanation: The resultant force vector is found by adding the corresponding components of the two vectors.

questions.q: What is the resultant force vector acting on the object?
questions.explanation: The resultant force vector acting on the object is (20i + 20j)

questions.q: What is the magnitude of the resultant force vector?
questions.explanation: The magnitude of the resultant force vector is √(20^2 + 20^2)

questions.q: What is the direction of the resultant force vector?
questions.explanation: The direction of the resultant force vector is Tan^-1(20/20)
